```markdown
# Implementation Prompt - Milestone 17 AI Code Recommendations And Agent Workflows

You are IBM Bob working in the RepoQuest repository.

Implement Milestone 17: **AI Code Recommendations And Agent Workflows**.

Milestones 15 and 16 should already be complete: optional assistant mode exists, AI Review is integrated into the product shell/workspace, mock/Claude/local/service providers use the same assistant interface, context packs are bounded, local model mode is supported, and AI-enhanced docs can be validated against deterministic evidence.

Your job is to make assistant mode feel like a real **code assistant**, not just a summarizer.

## Product Goal

RepoQuest should generate AI-assisted epics, tasks, workflows, test plans, documentation plans, and code recommendations that a developer or IBM Bob can act on.

AI recommendations must be grounded in RepoQuest evidence and clearly separated from deterministic findings.

## Required Baseline

Assume these are available:

- Deterministic task/workflow generator.
- Assistant provider interface with mock, Claude, local model, and async service paths.
- Bounded context packs.
- Citation validation.
- AI Review UX from Milestone 15.
- Local model support from Milestone 16.
- Component-based deterministic docs.
- Generated work plans and agent workflows.

Do not rebuild these foundations. Extend them into actionable recommendations.

## Required Outputs

Assistant mode should be able to generate:

- Application summary.
- Suggested epics and milestones.
- Task backlog.
- Agent workflows.
- Test plans.
- Component documentation plans.
- Code recommendation cards.
- Copyable AI Assistant actions.

These outputs must be shown as **AI-assisted** and must cite deterministic evidence.

## Recommendation Schema

Add or implement a schema equivalent to:

```python
 @dataclass
class CodeRecommendation:
  title: str
  category: str
  priority: str
  files: list[str]
  evidence: list[str]
  rationale: str
  proposed_change_summary: str
  test_plan: list[str]
  workflow: str
  confidence: float
```

Recommended categories:

- `testing`
- `documentation`
- `api`
- `frontend`
- `backend`
- `data_model`
- `error_handling`
- `refactor`
- `developer_experience`
- `unknown`

Recommended priorities:

- `high`
- `medium`
- `low`

## Guardrails

- Do not generate code patches in this milestone.
- Do not edit repository files based on AI output.
- Do not claim tests pass unless a verification command actually ran.
- Require evidence file paths for every recommendation.
- Mark AI suggestions as suggestions, not facts.
- Show deterministic tasks beside AI-enhanced tasks.
- Reject or downgrade uncited recommendations.
- Do not let AI output replace deterministic task/workflow generation.
- Do not send raw full repos to any model.

## Context Requirements

Use bounded context packs that include:

- Project fingerprint.
- Application graph summary.
- Test impact summary.
- Routes.
- Component cards.
- Reading path details.
- Deterministic tasks and workflows.
- Generated docs page summaries.
- Warnings and skipped files.
- Capped snippets for relevant files.

The AI should be asked to improve, prioritize, and synthesize from these deterministic findings, not rediscover the repo from scratch.

## UI Requirements

Add an **Assistant Recommendations** view only when assistant mode is enabled.

The view should:

- Show provider and model: mock, Claude, local model, or assistant service.
- Group recommendations by epic, task type, and priority.
- Show deterministic tasks next to AI-enhanced recommendations.
- Show source evidence next to every recommendation.
- Show validation status for AI outputs.
- Provide copyable agent workflows.
- Provide copyable AI Assistant actions / Bob-ready prompts.
- Clearly label AI-assisted content.
- Keep deterministic work plans usable when AI is disabled.

Suggested UI sections:

```text
Assistant Recommendations
- Provider status
- AI-generated epics
- Recommended task backlog
- Code recommendation cards
- Test plans
- Documentation plans
- Agent workflows
- Validation details
```

## Recommendation Card Requirements

Each AI recommendation card should show:

- Title.
- Category.
- Priority.
- Confidence.
- Files involved.
- Evidence citations.
- Rationale.
- Proposed change summary.
- Test plan.
- Agent workflow.
- Copyable prompt/action.
- Validation status.

Example card:

```text
Title: Add edge-case tests for trip deletion
Category: testing
Priority: high
Files: backend/routes/trips.py, backend/tests/test_trips.py
Evidence: DELETE /api/trips/{trip_id}
Rationale: Route has explicit 404 path for missing trip ids.
Proposed change: Add pytest case for deleting nonexistent trip.
Test plan: Run pytest for backend tests.
Workflow: Inspect route, add test, verify expected 404.
```

## Agent Workflow Requirements

AI-generated workflows should remain bounded and practical.

Each workflow should include:

- Goal.
- Files to read.
- Files likely to change.
- Ordered steps.
- Validation steps.
- Expected output.
- Copyable prompt.

Do not instruct RepoQuest to execute uploaded code. Validation steps are for the developer/Bob after export.

## Validation Rules

AI recommendation output must pass validation before being shown as trusted:

- Schema must parse.
- `files` must reference existing snapshot files.
- `evidence` must reference existing snapshot files or detected items.
- `confidence` must be within an expected range.
- `priority` must use allowed values.
- Empty rationale or proposed change summary is invalid.
- Recommendations without evidence are hidden or downgraded.
- Recommendations citing nonexistent files are rejected.
- Unsafe execution claims are rejected.

## Tests To Add Or Update

Add or update tests so these pass:

- Mock provider produces schema-valid workflows and code recommendations for the demo repo.
- Local-model mock/fixture path produces schema-valid workflows and recommendations.
- Every accepted AI recommendation links to existing file evidence.
- AI recommendation without evidence is not shown as trusted.
- AI output referencing nonexistent files is rejected.
- Invalid priority/category/confidence is rejected or normalized.
- Deterministic task generator still works when assistant mode is disabled.
- Assistant Recommendations view does not appear or shows disabled state when assistant mode is off.
- Provider/model metadata is displayed for AI-generated recommendations.
- Copyable workflows/prompts are generated from validated recommendation data.

Do not require live Claude, local model, or network calls in tests.

## Acceptance Criteria

Milestone 17 is complete when:

- Assistant mode feels like a code assistant, not only a summarizer.
- AI recommendations are actionable, evidence-linked, and visibly validated.
- Users can compare deterministic tasks with AI-enhanced recommendations.
- Users can copy agent workflows and Bob-ready prompts.
- Mock and local-model fixture paths are testable without network access.
- Invalid, uncited, or hallucinated AI output is rejected or clearly downgraded.
- Deterministic RepoQuest remains fully usable when AI is disabled.

## Out Of Scope

Do not generate patches.

Do not run tests automatically from uploaded repos.

Do not implement persistent recommendation storage beyond current session state.

Do not make AI recommendations required for export.

Do not hide deterministic findings behind assistant-generated content.
```

    "description": "This is a significant milestone that builds on the assistant foundation (M15) and context packs (M16) to make the assistant feel like a real code assistant that generates actionable recommendations.\n\nLet me analyze what needs to be done:\n\n**Current State (from previous milestones):**\n- M15: Optional assistant mode exists with mock/Claude/local/service providers\n- M16: Context packs are bounded, AI-enhanced docs validated against evidence\n- Deterministic task/workflow generator exists\n- Assistant provider interface is established\n\n**What This Milestone Adds:**\n1. **CodeRecommendation schema** - structured AI recommendations\n2. **AI-generated outputs**: epics, tasks, workflows, test plans, doc plans, code recommendations\n3. **Validation layer** - ensure AI recommendations cite real evidence\n4. **UI for Assistant Recommendations** - show AI suggestions alongside deterministic findings\n5. **Agent workflows** - copyable, bounded workflows for developers/Bob\n6. **Guardrails** - prevent hallucinations, require evidence, mark AI content clearly\n\n**Implementation Plan:**\n\n1. **Add CodeRecommendation model** to `repoquest/assistant_models.py`\n2. **Extend assistant provider interface** to generate recommendations\n3. **Add validation logic** for AI recommendations in `repoquest/assistant_validation.py`\n4. **Update mock provider** to generate valid recommendations for demo repo\n5. **Add recommendation generation** to assistant context/workflows\n6. **Update Streamlit UI** to show Assistant",

  },

    "description": "This is a significant milestone that builds on the assistant foundation (M15) and context packs (M16) to make the assistant feel like a real code assistant that generates actionable recommendations.\n\nLet me analyze what needs to be done:\n\n**Current State (from previous milestones):**\n- M15: Optional assistant mode exists with mock/Claude/local/service providers\n- M16: Context packs are bounded, AI-enhanced docs validated against evidence\n- Deterministic task/workflow generator exists\n- Assistant provider interface is established\n\n**What This Milestone Adds:**\n1. **CodeRecommendation schema** - structured AI recommendations\n2. **AI-generated outputs**: epics, tasks, workflows, test plans, doc plans, code recommendations\n3. **Validation layer** - ensure AI recommendations cite real evidence\n4. **UI for Assistant Recommendations** - show AI suggestions alongside deterministic findings\n5. **Agent workflows** - copyable, bounded workflows for developers/Bob\n6. **Guardrails** - prevent hallucinations, require evidence, mark AI content clearly\n\n**Implementation Plan:**\n\n1. **Add CodeRecommendation model** to `repoquest/assistant_models.py`\n2. **Extend assistant provider interface** to generate recommendations\n3. **Add validation logic** for AI recommendations in `repoquest/assistant_validation.py`\n4. **Update mock provider** to generate valid recommendations for demo repo\n5. **Add recommendation generation** to assistant context/workflows\n6. **Update Streamlit UI** to show Assistant Recommendations view\n7. **Add tests** for recommendation validation and generation\n8. **Update documentation** to explain AI recommendations feature\n\nLet me start implementing step by",

Task 5: Improve UI component accessibility\n  if frontend_components or frontend_pages:\n    ui_files = frontend_components + frontend_pages\n    evidence = [\n      f\"Found {len(ui_files)} UI files\",\n      \"Accessibility improves usability for all users\",\n    ]\n\n    tasks.append(TaskSuggestion(\n      epic=\"Accessibility & UX\",\n      priority=\"medium\",\n      files=ui_files[:5], # Limit to first 5\n      evidence=evidence,\n      why=\"Accessible components ensure the application is usable by everyone, including users with disabilities.\",\n      acceptance_criteria=[\n        \"ARIA labels added where needed\",\n        \"Keyboard navigation works\",\n        \"Color contrast meets standards\",\n        \"Screen reader friendly\",\n      ],\n      suggested_workflow=\"Accessibility audit and improvements\",\n    ))\n\n  # Task 7: Improve onboarding documentation\n  readme_files = _readme_paths(snapshot)\n  if readme_files:\n    evidence = [\n      \"README.md exists\",\n      f\"Project type: {fingerprint.project_type}\",\n    ]\n\n    tasks.append(TaskSuggestion(\n      epic=\"Documentation\",\n      priority=\"low\",\n      files=readme_files,\n      evidence=evidence,\n      why=\"Clear onboarding documentation helps new contributors get started quickly and reduces onboarding time.\",\n      acceptance_criteria=[\n        \"Setup instructions clear and complete\",\n        \"Architecture overview included\",\n        \"Common tasks documented\",\n        \"Troubleshooting section added\",\n      ],\n      suggested_workflow=\"Enhance README and onboarding docs\",\n    ))\n\n  return tasks\n\n\ndef generate_agent_workflows(\n  snapshot: RepositorySnapshot,\n  fingerprint: ProjectFingerprint,\n  routes: list[RouteInfo],\n  tasks: list[TaskSuggestion],\n  component_cards: list[ComponentCard],\n) -> list[AgentWorkflow]:\n  \"\"\"\n  Generate agent-ready workflows from tasks and repo analysis.\n\n  Args:\n    snapshot: Repository snapshot\n    fingerprint: Project fingerprint\n    routes: Detected API routes\n    tasks: Generated task suggestions\n    component_cards: Component cards\n\n  Returns:\n    List of AgentWorkflow objects\n  \"\"\"\n  workflows = []\n\n  backend_routes = _file_paths_by_role(snapshot, \"backend_route\")\n  test_files = _file_paths_by_role(snapshot, \"test\")\n  api_clients = _file_paths_by_role(snapshot, \"api_client\")\n\n  # Workflow 1: Add route edge-case tests\n  if backend_routes and routes:\n    route_file = backend_routes[0]\n    test_file = test_files[0] if test_files else f\"{route_file.replace('.py', '_test.py')}\"\n\n    route_list = [f\"{r.method} {r.path}\" for r in routes[:3]]\n\n    workflows.append(AgentWorkflow(\n      title=\"Add missing edge-case tests for API routes\",\n      goal=\"Ensure all API routes have comprehensive test coverage including edge cases\",\n      ordered_steps=[\n        \"Read the route file to understand endpoint implementations\",\n        \"Read existing test file to see current coverage\",\n        \"Identify missing test cases (invalid inputs, missing fields, edge cases)\",\n        \"Add new test functions for uncovered scenarios\",\n        \"Run pytest to verify all tests pass\",\n      ],\n      files_to_read=[route_file] + (test_files[:1] if test_files else []),\n      files_to_change=[test_file],\n      validation_steps=[\n        \"In a trusted local clone: Run pytest -v\",\n        \"Verify all new tests pass\",\n        \"Check test coverage increased\",\n      ],\n      expected_output=\"Updated test file with comprehensive edge-case coverage for all routes\",\n      prompt=f\"\"\"Analyze {route_file} which contains these routes: {', '.join(route_list)}.\n\nReview the existing tests in {test_file if test_files else 'the test directory'}.\n\nAdd pytest tests for these edge cases:\n1. Missing required fields\n2. Invalid data types\n3. Deleting non-existent resources\n4. Boundary conditions\n\nEnsure tests are clear, well-documented, and follow existing test patterns.\n\nDo not execute the uploaded code. Only analyze and generate tests.\"\"\",\n    ))\n\n  # Workflow 2: Improve API client error handling\n  if api_clients:\n    api_client = api_clients[0]\n\n    workflows.append(AgentWorkflow(\n      title=\"Improve frontend API client error handling\",\n      goal=\"Add robust error handling, timeouts, and user-friendly error messages to API client\",\n      ordered_steps=[\n        \"Read the API client file to understand current implementation\",\n        \"Identify API calls without proper error handling\",\n        \"Add try-catch blocks with specific error types\",\n        \"Add timeout handling\",\n        \"Add user-friendly error messages\",\n        \"Consider adding retry logic for transient failures\",\n      ],\n      files_to_read=[api_client],\n      files_to_change=[api_client],\n      validation_steps=[\n        \"For the developer's own trusted repo: Test with network disconnected\",\n        \"Test with slow network (timeout scenarios)\",\n        \"Test with API returning errors\",\n        \"Verify user sees helpful error messages\",\n      ],\n      expected_output=\"API client with comprehensive error handling and user-friendly messages\",\n      prompt=f\"\"\"Review {api_client} and improve error handling.\n\nAdd:\n1. Try-catch blocks for all API calls\n2. Timeout handling (e.g., 10 second timeout)\n3. Specific error types (NetworkError, TimeoutError, APIError)\n4. User-friendly error messages\n5. Optional: Retry logic for transient failures\n\nMaintain existing functionality while adding robustness.\n\nDo not execute the uploaded code. Only analyze and suggest improvements.\"\"\",\n    ))\n\n  # Workflow 3: Document API routes\n  if backend_routes and routes:\n    readme_files = _readme_paths(snapshot)\n    readme_file = readme_files[0] if readme_files else \"README.md\"\n\n    route_list = \"\\n\".join([f\"- {r.method} {r.path}\" for r in routes[:5]])\n\n    workflows.append(AgentWorkflow(\n      title=\"Generate API documentation\",\n      goal=\"Create comprehensive API documentation for all detected routes\",\n      ordered_steps=[\n        \"Read route files to understand endpoint implementations\",\n        \"Extract request/response schemas\",\n        \"Document each endpoint with method, path, parameters, and responses\",\n        \"Add example requests and responses\",\n        \"Update README.md with API documentation section\",\n      ],\n      files_to_read=backend_routes[:3],\n      files_to_change=[readme_file],\n      validation_steps=[\n        \"Verify all routes documented\",\n        \"Check examples are accurate\",\n        \"Ensure formatting is clear\",\n      ],\n      expected_output=\"README.md with complete API documentation section\",\n      prompt=f\"\"\"Analyze these route files: {', '.join(backend_routes[:3])}.\n\nDetected routes:\n{route_list}\n\nGenerate API documentation including:\n1. Endpoint list with HTTP methods and paths\n2. Request parameters and body schemas\n3. Response formats\n4. Example requests using curl or similar\n5. Error responses\n\nAdd this documentation to {readme_file} in a new \"API Documentation\" section.\n\nDo not execute the uploaded code. Only analyze and generate documentation.\"\"\",\n    ))\n\n  return workflows\n\n\ndef group_tasks_into_milestones(tasks: list[TaskSuggestion]) -> list[SuggestedMilestone]:\n  \"\"\"\n  Group tasks into logical milestones based on epics.\n\n  Args:\n    tasks: List of task suggestions\n\n  Returns:\n    List of SuggestedMilestone objects\n  \"\"\"\n  # Group tasks by epic\n  epic_tasks: dict[str, list[TaskSuggestion]] = {}\n  for task in tasks:\n    if task.epic not in epic_tasks:\n      epic_tasks[task.epic] = []\n    epic_tasks[task.epic].append(task)\n\n  milestones = []\n\n  # Create milestone for each epic\n  for epic, epic_task_list in epic_tasks.items():\n    # Sort by priority\n    priority_order = {\"high\": 0, \"medium\": 1, \"low\": 2}\n    epic_task_list.sort(key=lambda t: priority_order.get(t.priority, 3))\n\n    milestones.append(SuggestedMilestone(\n      title=f\"Milestone: {epic}\",\n      goal=get_work_plan_epic_goal(epic),\n      tasks=epic_task_list,\n    ))\n\n  # Sort milestones by priority (based on highest priority task in each)\n  def milestone_priority(m: SuggestedMilestone) -> int:\n    priority_order = {\"high\": 0, \"medium\": 1, \"low\": 2}\n    return min(priority_order.get(t.priority, 3) for t in m.tasks)\n\n  milestones.sort(key=milestone_priority)\n\n  return milestones\n\n\ndef generate_work_plan(\n  snapshot: RepositorySnapshot,\n  fingerprint: ProjectFingerprint,\n  routes: list[RouteInfo],\n  import_edges: list[ImportEdge],\n  reading_path: list[ReadingPathItem],\n  component_cards: list[ComponentCard],\n) -> WorkPlan:\n  \"\"\"\n  Generate a complete work plan with epics, tasks, milestones, and workflows.\n\n  Args:\n    snapshot: Repository snapshot\n    fingerprint: Project fingerprint\n    routes: Detected API routes\n    import_edges: Import relationships\n    reading_path: Suggested reading path\n    component_cards: Component cards\n\n  Returns:\n    WorkPlan object\n  \"\"\"\n  # Generate tasks\n  tasks = generate_task_suggestions(\n    snapshot, fingerprint, routes, import_edges, reading_path, component_cards\n  )\n\n  # Extract unique epics\n  epics = list(dict.fromkeys(task.epic for task in tasks))\n\n  # Generate workflows\n  workflows = generate_agent_workflows(\n    snapshot, fingerprint, routes, tasks, component_cards\n  )\n\n  # Group into milestones\n  milestones = group_tasks_into_milestones(tasks)\n\n  return WorkPlan(\n    epics=epics,\n    tasks=tasks,\n    milestones=milestones,\n    workflows=workflows,\n  )\n\n\ndef export_workflows_markdown(work_plan: WorkPlan, project_name: str) -> str:\n  \"\"\"\n  Export work plan as Markdown.\n\n  Args:\n    work_plan: WorkPlan object\n    project_name: Name of the project\n\n  Returns:\n    Markdown string\n  \"\"\"\n  lines = [\n    f\"# RepoQuest Work Plan: {project_name}\",\n    \"\",\n    \"This work plan was generated by RepoQuest based on deterministic repository analysis.\",\n    \"\",\n    \"## Summary\",\n    \"\",\n    f\"- **Epics**: {len(work_plan.epics)}\",\n    f\"- **Tasks**: {len(work_plan.tasks)}\",\n    f\"- **Milestones**: {len(work_plan.milestones)}\",\n    f\"- **Agent Workflows**: {len(work_plan.workflows)}\",\n    \"\",\n  ]\n\n  # Epics\n  lines.extend([\n    \"## Epics\",\n    \"\",\n  ])\n  for epic in work_plan.epics:\n    epic_tasks = [t for t in work_plan.tasks if t.epic == epic]\n    lines.append(f\"- **{epic}** ({len(epic_tasks)} tasks)\")\n  lines.append(\"\")\n\n  # Milestones\n  lines.extend([\n    \"## Suggested Milestones\",\n    \"\",\n  ])\n  for milestone in work_plan.milestones:\n    lines.extend([\n      f\"### {milestone.title}\",\n      \"\",\n      f\"**Goal**: {milestone.goal}\",\n      \"\",\n      f\"**Tasks** ({len(milestone.tasks)}):\",\n      \"\",\n    ])\n    for task in milestone.tasks:\n      lines.append(f\"- [{task.priority.upper()}] {task.why}\")\n    lines.append(\"\")\n\n  # Tasks\n  lines.extend([\n    \"## Tasks\",\n    \"\",\n  ])\n  for task in work_plan.tasks:\n    lines.extend([\n      f\"### {task.epic}: {task.why[:80]}...\",\n      \"\",\n      f\"**Priority**: {task.priority}\",\n      \"\",\n      \"**Files**:\",\n      \"\",\n    ])\n    for file in task.files[:5]:\n      lines.append(f\"- `{file}`\")\n    lines.extend([\n      \"\",\n      \"**Evidence**:\",\n      \"\",\n    ])\n    for evidence in task.evidence:\n      lines.append(f\"- {evidence}\")\n    lines.extend([\n      \"\",\n      \"**Why it matters**:\",\n      \"\",\n      task.why,\n      \"\",\n      \"**Acceptance Criteria**:\",\n      \"\",\n    ])\n    for criterion in task.acceptance_criteria:\n      lines.append(f\"- {criterion}\")\n    lines.extend([\n      \"\",\n      \"---\",\n      \"\",\n    ])\n\n  # Workflows\n  lines.extend([\n    \"## Agent Workflows\",\n    \"\",\n    \"These workflows are ready to use with an AI coding assistant.\",\n    \"\",\n  ])\n  for workflow in work_plan.workflows:\n    lines.extend([\n      f\"### Workflow: {workflow.title}\",\n      \"\",\n      f\"**Goal**: {workflow.goal}\",\n      \"\",\n      \"**Files to read**:\",\n      \"\",\n    ])\n    for file in workflow.files_to_read:\n      lines.append(f\"- `{file}`\")\n    lines.extend([\n      \"\",\n      \"**Files likely to change**:\",\n      \"\",\n    ])\n    for file in workflow.files_to_change:\n      lines.append(f\"- `{file}`\")\n    lines.extend([\n      \"\",\n      \"**Ordered steps**:\",\n      \"\",\n    ])\n    for i, step in enumerate(workflow.ordered_steps, 1):\n      lines.append(f\"{i}. {step}\")\n    lines.extend([\n      \"\",\n      \"**Validation steps**:\",\n      \"\",\n    ])\n    for step in workflow.validation_steps:\n      lines.append(f\"- {step}\")\n    lines.extend([\n      \"\",\n      f\"**Expected output**: {workflow.expected_output}\",\n      \"\",\n      \"**AI Assistant Action**:\",\n      \"\",\n      \"```\",\n      workflow.prompt,\n      \"```\",\n      \"\",\n      \"---\",\n      \"\",\n    ])\n\n  return \"\\n\".join(lines)\n\n# Made with Bob\n"
          }

Timestamp: 2026-05-16T16:35:26.854Z

<thinking>**Understanding Milestone 17 requirements**

I need to implement AI Code Recommendations and Agent Workflows for RepoQuest. Looking at the existing code:

1. `assistant_models.py` has basic models but lacks `CodeRecommendation` schema
2. `workflows.py` has deterministic task/workflow generation but no AI-enhanced recommendations

Key requirements:
- Add `CodeRecommendation` dataclass with validation
- Create AI recommendation generation logic
- Add validation for AI outputs (file existence, evidence, schema)
- Create UI components for Assistant Recommendations view
- Ensure AI recommendations cite deterministic evidence
- Keep deterministic workflows usable when AI is disabled
- Add tests for validation and mock providers

Let me start by creating a todo list and then implementing the core recommendation models and validation.
</thinking>

    "description": "Replace the entire TODO list with an updated checklist reflecting the current state. Always provide the full list; the system will overwrite the previous one. This tool is designed for step-by-step task tracking, allowing you to confirm completion of each step before updating, update multiple task statuses at once (e.g., mark one as completed and start the next), and dynamically add new todos discovered during long or complex tasks.\n\n**Checklist Format:**\n- Use a single-level markdown checklist (no nesting or subtasks).\n- List todos in the intended execution order.\n- Status options:\n\t - [ ] Task description (pending)\n\t - [x] Task description (completed)\n\t - [-] Task description (in progress)\n\n**Status Rules:**\n- [ ] = pending (not started)\n- [x] = completed (fully finished, no unresolved issues)\n- [-] = in_progress (currently being worked on)\n\n**Core Principles:**\n- Before updating, always confirm which todos have been completed since the last update.\n- You may update multiple statuses in a single update (e.g., mark the previous as completed and the next as in progress).\n- When a new actionable item is discovered during a long or complex task, add it to the todo list immediately.\n- Do not remove any unfinished todos unless explicitly instructed.\n- Always retain all unfinished tasks, updating their status as needed.\n- Only mark a task as completed when it is fully accomplished (no partials, no unresolved dependencies).\n- If a task is blocked, keep it as in_progress and add a new todo describing what needs to be resolved.\n- Remove tasks only if they are no longer relevant or if the user requests deletion.\n\n**Usage Example:**\n<update_todo_list>\n<todos>\n[x] Analyze requirements\n[x] Design architecture\n[-] Implement core logic\n[ ] Write tests\n[ ] Update documentation\n</todos>\n</update_todo_list>\n\n*After completing \"Implement core logic\" and starting \"Write tests\":*\n<update_todo_list>\n<todos>\n[x] Analyze requirements\n[x] Design architecture\n[x] Implement core logic\n[-] Write tests\n[ ] Update documentation\n[ ] Add performance benchmarks\n</todos>\n</update_todo_list>\n\n**When to Use:**\n- The task is complicated or involves multiple steps or requires ongoing tracking.\n- You need to update the status of several todos at once.\n- New actionable items are discovered during task execution.\n- The user requests a todo list or provides multiple tasks.\n- The task is complex and benefits from clear, stepwise progress tracking.\n\n**When NOT to Use:**\n- There is only a single, trivial task.\n- The task can be completed in one or two simple steps.\n- The request is purely conversational or informational.\n\n**Task Management Guidelines:**\n- Mark task as completed immediately after all work of the current task is done.\n- Start the next task by marking it as in_progress.\n- Add new todos as soon as they are identified.\n- Use clear, descriptive task names.\n",
    "renderOutputAsMarkdown": true
  }
]

is_valid(self) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"Check if recommendation passed validation.\"\"\"\n    return self.validation_status == \"valid\"\n\n  @property\n  def is_trusted(self) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"Check if def from_dict(cls, data: dict[str, object]) -> \"CodeRecommendation\":\n    \"\"\"Build a recommendation from JSON.\"\"\"\n    files = data.get(\"files\", [])\n    evidence = data.get(\"evidence\",   )\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass AIRecommendationResult:\n  \"\"\"Result of AI recommendation generation.\"\"\"\n  recommendations: list[CodeRecommendation]\n  provider: str\n  model: str\n  context_pack: IMPORTANT: NEVER use apply_diff to replace large parts or whole file contents of a file!! use write_to_file instead.\n      \n      Diff format:\n      ```\n# Search: |||\n[exact content to find including whitespace]\n|||\n# Start Line: [optional line number where search should begin]\n# Replace with: |||\n[new content to replace with]\n|||\n# Purpose: [optional explanation of what this replacement does]\n\n# Search: |||\n[another exact content to find]\n|||\n# Start Line: [optional line number]\n# Replace with: |||\n[replacement content]\n|||\n```\n\nFor multiline search or replacements, use triple |||, use another if content contains that delimiter:\n\n```\n# Search: |||\nfunction example() {\n  const x = 1;\n  const y = 2;\n  return x + y;\n}\n|||\n# Start Line: 10\n# Replace with: |||\nfunction example() {\n  const x = 1;\n  const y = 2;\n  console.log('Computing sum');\n  return x + y;\n}\n|||\n# Purpose: Add logging to the function\n```\n\nExample:\n\nOriginal file:\n```\n1 | def calculate_total(items):\n2 |     total = 0\n3 |     for item in items:\n4 |         total += item\n5 |     return total\n```\n\nSearch/Replace content:\n```\n# Search: |||\ntotal += item\n|||\n# Start Line: 3\n# Replace with: |||\ntotal = total + item\n|||\n# Purpose: Make addition more explicit\n\n# Search: |||\ndef calculate_total(items):\n|||\n# Replace with: |||\ndef calculate_sum(items):\n|||\n# Purpose: Rename function to better reflect its purpose\n```\n\nUsage:\n<apply_diff>\n<file_path>File path here</file_path>\n<diff>\nYour search/replace content here\nYou can use multiple search/replace blocks in one diff block\n</diff>\n</apply_diff>\n",

      "content": "\"\"\"Validation logic for AI-generated code recommendations.\"\"\"\n\nfrom repoquest.assistant_models import CodeRecommendation\nfrom repoquest.models import RepositorySnapshot\n\n\nVALID_CATEGORIES = {\n    \"testing\",\n    \"documentation\",\n    \"api\",\n    \"frontend\",\n    \"backend\",\n    \"data_model\",\n    \"error_handling\",\n    \"refactor\",\n    \"developer_experience\",\n    \"unknown\",\n}\n\nVALID_PRIORITIES = {\"high\", \"medium\", \"low\"}\n\n\ndef validate_recommendation(\n    recommendation: CodeRecommendation,\n    snapshot: RepositorySnapshot,\n) -> CodeRecommendation:\n    \"\"\"\n    Validate an AI-generated recommendation against repository evidence.\n\n    Args:\n        recommendation: The recommendation to validate\n        snapshot: Repository snapshot for evidence checking\n\n    Returns:\n        Updated recommendation with validation status and warnings\n    \"\"\"\n    warnings = []\n    \n    # Build set of valid file paths from snapshot\n    valid_files = {file_info.path for file_info in snapshot.files}\n    \n    # Validate category\n    if recommendation.category not in VALID_CATEGORIES:\n        warnings.append(f\"Invalid category '{recommendation.category}', normalized to 'unknown'\")\n        recommendation.category = \"unknown\"\n    \n    # Validate priority\n    if recommendation.priority not in VALID_PRIORITIES:\n        warnings.append(f\"Invalid priority '{recommendation.priority}', normalized to 'low'\")\n        recommendation.priority = \"low\"\n    \n    # Validate confidence range\n    if not (0.0 <= recommendation.confidence <= 1.0):\n        warnings.append(f\"Confidence {recommendation.confidence} out of range [0.0, 1.0], clamped\")\n        recommendation.confidence = max(0.0, min(1.0, recommendation.confidence))\n    \n    # Validate required fields\n    if not recommendation.title:\n        warnings.append(\"Missing title\")\n        recommendation.validation_status = \"invalid\"\n        recommendation.validation_warnings = warnings\n        return recommendation\n    \n    if not recommendation.rationale:\n        warnings.append(\"Missing rationale\")\n        recommendation.validation_status = \"invalid\"\n        recommendation.validation_warnings = warnings\n        return recommendation\n    \n    if not recommendation.proposed_change_summary:\n        warnings.append(\"Missing proposed change summary\")\n        recommendation.validation_status = \"invalid\"\n        recommendation.validation_warnings = warnings\n        return recommendation\n    \n    # Validate files exist in snapshot\n    invalid_files = []\n    for file_path in recommendation.files:\n        if file_path not in valid_files:\n            invalid_files.append(file_path)\n    \n    if invalid_files:\n        warnings.append(f\"Referenced {len(invalid_files)} nonexistent files: {', '.join(invalid_files[:3])}\")\n        # Remove invalid files\n        recommendation.files = [f for f in recommendation.files if f in valid_files]\n    \n    # Validate evidence references\n    invalid_evidence = []\n    for evidence_item in recommendation.evidence:\n        # Check if evidence references a file path\n        if \"/\" in evidence_item or \"\\\\\" in evidence_item:\n            # Extract potential file path\n            parts = evidence_item.split()\n            for part in parts:\n                if \"/\" in part or \"\\\\\" in part:\n                    # Clean up common prefixes/suffixes\n                    clean_part = part.strip(\"'\\\"`:,\")\n                    if clean_part and clean_part not in valid_files:\n                        invalid_evidence.append(clean_part)\n    \n    if invalid_evidence:\n        warnings.append(f\"Evidence references {len(invalid_evidence)} nonexistent files\")\n    \n    # Check for evidence\n    if not recommendation.evidence:\n        warnings.append(\"No evidence provided\")\n        recommendation.validation_status = \"downgraded\"\n        recommendation.validation_warnings = warnings\n        return recommendation\n    \n    # Check for files\n    if not recommendation.files:\n        warnings.append(\"No files specified\")\n        recommendation.validation_status = \"downgraded\"\n        recommendation.validation_warnings = warnings\n        return recommendation\n    \n    # Determine final validation status\n    if warnings:\n        if any(\"Missing\" in w or \"nonexistent files\" in w for w in warnings):\n            recommendation.validation_status = \"downgraded\"\n        else:\n            recommendation.validation_status = \"valid\"\n    else:\n        recommendation.validation_status = \"valid\"\n    \n    recommendation.validation_warnings = warnings\n    return recommendation\n\n\ndef validate_recommendations(\n    recommendations: list[CodeRecommendation],\n    snapshot: RepositorySnapshot,\n) -> list[CodeRecommendation]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Validate a list of recommendations.\n\n    Args:\n        recommendations: List of recommendations to validate\n        snapshot: Repository snapshot for evidence checking\n\n    Returns:\n        List of validated recommendations\n    \"\"\"\n    return [validate_recommendation(rec, snapshot) for rec in recommendations]\n\n\ndef filter_trusted_recommendations(\n    recommendations: list[CodeRecommendation],\n) -> list[CodeRecommendation]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Filter recommendations to only those that are trusted (valid or downgraded).\n\n    Args:\n        recommendations: List of recommendations\n\n    Returns:\n        List of trusted recommendations\n    \"\"\"\n    return [r for r in recommendations if r.is_trusted]\n\n\ndef group_recommendations_by_category(\n    recommendations: list[CodeRecommendation],\n) -> dict[str, list[CodeRecommendation]]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Group recommendations by category.\n\n    Args:\n        recommendations: List of recommendations\n\n    Returns:\n        Dictionary mapping category to list of recommendations\n    \"\"\"\n    grouped: dict[str, list[CodeRecommendation]] = {}\n    for rec in recommendations:\n        if rec.category not in grouped:\n            grouped[rec.category] = []\n        grouped[rec.category].append(rec)\n    return grouped\n\n\ndef group_recommendations_by_priority(\n    recommendations: list[CodeRecommendation],\n) -> dict[str, list[CodeRecommendation]]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Group recommendations by priority.\n\n    Args:\n        recommendations: List of recommendations\n\n    Returns:\n        Dictionary mapping priority to list of recommendations\n    \"\"\"\n    grouped: dict[str, list[CodeRecommendation]] = {}\n    for rec in recommendations:\n        if rec.priority not in grouped:\n            grouped[rec.priority] = []\n        grouped[rec.priority].append(rec)\n    return grouped\n\n\ndef sort_recommendations_by_priority(\n    recommendations: list[CodeRecommendation],\n) -> list[CodeRecommendation]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Sort recommendations by priority (high -> medium -> low).\n\n    Args:\n        recommendations: List of recommendations\n\n    Returns:\n        Sorted list of recommendations\n    \"\"\"\n    priority_order = {\"high\": 0, \"medium\": 1, \"low\": 2}\n    return sorted(\n        recommendations,\n        key=lambda r: (priority_order.get(r.priority, 3), -r.confidence)\n    )\n"
    },

This tool is designed for step-by-step task tracking, allowing you to confirm completion of each step before updating, update multiple task statuses at once (e.g., mark one as completed and start the next), and dynamically add new todos discovered during long or complex tasks.\n\n**Checklist Format:**\n- Use a single-level markdown checklist (no nesting or subtasks).\n- List todos in the intended execution order.\n- Status options:\n\t - [ ] Task description (pending)\n\t - [x] Task description (completed)\n\t - [-] Task description (in progress)\n\n**Status Rules:**\n- [ ] = pending (not started)\n- [x] = completed (fully finished, no unresolved issues)\n- [-] = in_progress (currently being worked on)\n\n**Core Principles:**\n- Before updating, always confirm which todos have been completed since the last update.\n- You may update multiple statuses in a single update (e.g., mark the previous as completed and the next as in progress).\n- When a new actionable item is discovered during a long or complex task, add it to the todo list immediately.\n- Do not remove any unfinished todos unless explicitly instructed.\n- Always retain all unfinished tasks, updating their status as needed.\n- Only mark a task as completed when it is fully accomplished (no partials, no unresolved dependencies).\n- If a task is blocked, keep it as in_progress and add a new todo describing what needs to be resolved.\n- Remove tasks only if they are no longer relevant or if the user requests deletion.\n\n**Usage Example:**\n<update_todo_list>\n<todos>\n[x] Analyze requirements\n[x] Design architecture\n[-] Implement core logic\n[ ] Write tests\n[ ] Update documentation\n</todos>\n</update_todo_list>\n\n*After completing \"Implement core logic\" and starting \"Write tests\":*\n<update_todo_list>\n<todos>\n[x] Analyze requirements\n[x] Design architecture\n[x] Implement core logic\n[-] Write tests\n[ ] Update documentation\n[ ] Add performance benchmarks\n</todos>\n</update_todo_list>\n\n**When to Use:**\n- The task is complicated or involves multiple steps or requires ongoing tracking.\n- You need to update the status of several todos at once.\n- New actionable items are discovered during task execution.\n- The user requests a todo list or provides multiple tasks.\n- The task is complex and benefits from clear, stepwise progress tracking.\n\n**When NOT to Use:**\n- There is only a single, trivial task.\n- The task can be completed in one or two simple steps.\n- The request is purely conversational or informational.\n\n**Task Management Guidelines:**\n- Mark task as completed immediately after all work of the current task is done.\n- Start the next task by marking it as in_progress.\n- Add new todos as soon as they are identified.\n- Use clear, descriptive task names.\n",
    "renderOutputAsMarkdown": true
  }
]

<details>
<summary>Tool calls</summary>

```json
[
  {
    "id": "tool-7",
    "name": "write_to_file",
    "args": {
      "file_path": "/home/ovidiu/Projects/bob/proj/repoquest/recommendation_generator.py",
      "content": "\"\"\"Generate AI-assisted code recommendations from repository analysis.\"\"\"\n\nfrom repoquest.assistant_models import (\n    CodeRecommendation,\n    AIRecommendationResult,\n    ContextPack,\n)\nfrom repoquest.models import (\n    RepositorySnapshot,\n    ProjectFingerprint,\n    RouteInfo,\n    ComponentCard,\n    WorkPlan,\n)\nfrom repoquest.recommendation_validator import validate_recommendations\n\n\ndef generate_mock_recommendations(\n    snapshot: RepositorySnapshot,\n    fingerprint: ProjectFingerprint,\n    routes: list[RouteInfo],\n    component_cards: list[ComponentCard],\n    work_plan: WorkPlan,\n    context_pack: ContextPack,\n) -> AIRecommendationResult:\n    \"\"\"\n    Generate mock AI recommendations for testing and demo purposes.\n\n    This produces deterministic recommendations based on repo analysis\n    without requiring an actual AI model.\n\n    Args:\n        snapshot: Repository snapshot\n        fingerprint: Project fingerprint\n        routes: Detected routes\n        component_cards: Component cards\n        work_plan: Generated work plan\n        context_pack: Context pack used for generation\n\n    Returns:\n        AIRecommendationResult with mock recommendations\n    \"\"\"\n    recommendations = []\n    \n    # Find relevant files\n    backend_routes = [f.path for f in snapshot.files if f.role == \"backend_route\" and not f.skipped]\n    test_files = [f.path for f in snapshot.files if f.role == \"test\" and not f.skipped]\n    api_clients = [f.path for f in snapshot.files if f.role == \"api_client\" and not f.skipped]\n    models = [f.path for f in snapshot.files if f.role == \"model\" and not f.skipped]\n    \n    # Recommendation 1: Add edge-case tests for routes\n    if backend_routes and routes:\n        route_file = backend_routes[0]\n        test_file = test_files[0] if test_files else f\"{route_file.replace('.py', '_test.py')}\"\n        \n        route_list = [f\"{r.method} {r.path}\" for r in routes[:3]]\n        \n        recommendations.append(CodeRecommendation(\n            title=\"Add edge-case tests for API route deletion\",\n            category=\"testing\",\n            priority=\"high\",\n            files=[route_file, test_file],\n            evidence=[\n                f\"Route file: {route_file}\",\n                f\"Detected routes: {', '.join(route_list)}\",\n                \"DELETE endpoint found with explicit 404 handling\",\n            ],\n            rationale=\"The DELETE route has explicit error handling for missing resources (404 response). This edge case should be tested to ensure the API behaves correctly when clients attempt to delete nonexistent items.\",\n            proposed_change_summary=\"Add a pytest test case that attempts to delete a nonexistent resource and verifies a 404 response is returned with an appropriate error message.\",\n            test_plan=[\n                \"Add test function test_delete_nonexistent_trip()\",\n                \"Mock or use test database without the target resource\",\n                \"Call DELETE endpoint with nonexistent ID\",\n                \"Assert response status is 404\",\n                \"Assert error message is clear and helpful\",\n                \"Run pytest -v to verify test passes\",\n            ],\n            workflow=\"Inspect route implementation -> Review existing tests -> Add new test case -> Verify test passes\",\n            confidence=0.85,\n        ))\n    \n    # Recommendation 2: Improve API client error handling\n    if api_clients:\n        api_client = api_clients[0]\n        \n        recommendations.append(CodeRecommendation(\n            title=\"Add timeout and retry logic to API client\",\n            category=\"error_handling\",\n            priority=\"high\",\n            files=[api_client],\n            evidence=[\n                f\"API client: {api_client}\",\n                \"No timeout configuration detected\",\n                \"No retry logic for transient failures\",\n            ],\n            rationale=\"The API client currently lacks timeout handling and retry logic. This can lead to poor user experience when network conditions are unstable or the backend is temporarily unavailable. Adding these features improves reliability.\",\n            proposed_change_summary=\"Add a 10-second timeout to all API calls and implement exponential backoff retry logic (max 3 retries) for transient failures like network errors or 5xx responses.\",\n            test_plan=[\n                \"Add timeout parameter to fetch/axios calls\",\n                \"Implement retry wrapper with exponential backoff\",\n                \"Test with simulated slow network\",\n                \"Test with simulated network failure\",\n                \"Verify user sees helpful error messages\",\n            ],\n            workflow=\"Review current API client -> Add timeout config -> Implement retry logic -> Add error messages -> Test edge cases\",\n            confidence=0.90,\n        ))\n    \n    # Recommendation 3: Add input validation to data models\n    if models:\n        model_file = models[0]\n        \n        recommendations.append(CodeRecommendation(\n            title=\"Add Pydantic validation to data models\",\n            category=\"data_model\",\n            priority=\"medium\",\n            files=[model_file],\n            evidence=[\n                f\"Model file: {model_file}\",\n                \"Data models define structure but lack validation\",\n                \"No constraints on required fields or data types\",\n            ],\n            rationale=\"Data models currently lack validation constraints. Adding Pydantic validators ensures data integrity at the model level, catching invalid data before it enters the system and providing clear error messages.\",\n            proposed_change_summary=\"Convert data models to Pydantic BaseModel classes with field validators for required fields, data types, string lengths, and business logic constraints.\",\n            test_plan=[\n                \"Add Pydantic BaseModel inheritance\",\n                \"Add Field validators for constraints\",\n                \"Add custom validators for business logic\",\n                \"Write unit tests for valid and invalid data\",\n                \"Verify validation errors are clear\",\n            ],\n            workflow=\"Review model structure -> Add Pydantic validators -> Add constraint checks -> Write validation tests -> Verify error messages\",\n            confidence=0.80,\n        ))\n    \n    # Recommendation 4: Document API endpoints\n    if backend_routes and routes:\n        readme_files = [f.path for f in snapshot.files if f.name.lower() == \"readme.md\" and not f.skipped]\n        readme_file = readme_files[0] if readme_files else \"README.md\"\n        \n        recommendations.append(CodeRecommendation(\n            title=\"Generate comprehensive API documentation\",\n            category=\"documentation\",\n            priority=\"medium\",\n            files=[readme_file] + backend_routes[:2],\n            evidence=[\n                f\"Detected {len(routes)} API routes\",\n                f\"Route files: {', '.join(backend_routes[:2])}\",\n                \"README exists but lacks API documentation section\",\n            ],\n            rationale=\"The API currently lacks comprehensive documentation. Adding detailed endpoint documentation helps developers understand available endpoints, request/response formats, and usage examples, reducing integration time.\",\n            proposed_change_summary=\"Add an 'API Documentation' section to README.md with all endpoints, HTTP methods, request parameters, response schemas, and example curl commands.\",\n            test_plan=[\n                \"Document each endpoint with method and path\",\n                \"Add request parameter descriptions\",\n                \"Add response schema examples\",\n                \"Add curl example for each endpoint\",\n                \"Review for accuracy and completeness\",\n            ],\n            workflow=\"Extract route details -> Document request/response schemas -> Add examples -> Update README -> Review\",\n            confidence=0.75,\n        ))\n    \n    # Recommendation 5: Add integration tests\n    if backend_routes and test_files:\n        recommendations.append(CodeRecommendation(\n            title=\"Add end-to-end integration tests\",\n            category=\"testing\",\n            priority=\"medium\",\n            files=test_files[:2] + backend_routes[:2],\n            evidence=[\n                f\"Found {len(test_files)} test files\",\n                \"Unit tests exist but no integration tests detected\",\n                \"Multiple routes that interact with each other\",\n            ],\n            rationale=\"While unit tests exist, integration tests are needed to verify that components work together correctly. This catches issues that only appear when the full system is running.\",\n            proposed_change_summary=\"Add integration test suite that starts the application, makes real HTTP requests to endpoints, and verifies end-to-end behavior including database interactions.\",\n            test_plan=[\n                \"Set up test database/fixtures\",\n                \"Create integration test file\",\n                \"Add tests for common user workflows\",\n                \"Test error scenarios across components\",\n                \"Verify cleanup after tests\",\n            ],\n            workflow=\"Design test scenarios -> Set up test environment -> Write integration tests -> Run and verify -> Add to CI\",\n            confidence=0.70,\n        ))\n    \n    # Validate all recommendations\n    validated = validate_recommendations(recommendations, snapshot)\n    \n    return AIRecommendationResult(\n        recommendations=validated,\n        provider=\"mock\",\n        model=\"deterministic-v1\",\n        context_pack=context_pack,\n        warnings=[],\n    )\n\n\ndef create_recommendation_prompt(\n    context_pack: ContextPack,\n    work_plan: WorkPlan,\n) -> str:\n    \"\"\"\n    Create a prompt for AI recommendation generation.\n\n    Args:\n        context_pack: Bounded context pack\n        work_plan: Generated work plan\n\n    Returns:\n        Prompt string for AI model\n    \"\"\"\n    lines = [\n        \"# Code Recommendation Task\",\n        \"\",\n        \"You are an expert software engineer reviewing a codebase.\",\n        \"\",\n        \"## Repository Context\",\n        \"\",\n        f\"**Project Summary**: {context_pack.project_summary}\",\n        \"\",\n        f\"**Frameworks**: {', '.join(context_pack.frameworks)}\",\n        \"\",\n        f\"**Entry Points**: {', '.join(context_pack.entry_points[:5])}\",\n        \"\",\n        \"## Detected Components\",\n        \"\",\n        context_pack.component_summary,\n        \"\",\n        \"## Detected Routes\",\n        \"\",\n        context_pack.routes_summary,\n        \"\",\n        \"## Test Coverage\",\n        \"\",\n        context_pack.test_summary,\n        \"\",\n        \"## Deterministic Work Plan\",\n        \"\",\n        context_pack.workflow_summary,\n        \"\",\n        \"## Your Task\",\n        \"\",\n        \"Generate 3-5 high-value code recommendations that:\",\n        \"\",\n        \"1. Are grounded in the evidence above\",\n        \"2. Reference specific files from the repository\",\n        \"3. Provide clear rationale and proposed changes\",\n        \"4. Include actionable test plans\",\n        \"5. Suggest practical workflows\",\n        \"\",\n        \"## Output Format\",\n        \"\",\n        \"Return a JSON array of recommendations with this schema:\",\n        \"\",\n        \"```json\",\n        \"[\",\n        \"  {\",\n        '    \"title\": \"Clear, specific title\",',\n        '    \"category\": \"testing|documentation|api|frontend|backend|data_model|error_handling|refactor|developer_experience\",',\n        '    \"priority\": \"high|medium|low\",',\n        '    \"files\": [\"file1.py\", \"file2.ts\"],',\n        '    \"evidence\": [\"Evidence item 1\", \"Evidence item 2\"],',\n        '    \"rationale\": \"Why this matters\",',\n        '    \"proposed_change_summary\": \"What to change\",',\n        '    \"test_plan\": [\"Step 1\", \"Step 2\"],',\n        '    \"workflow\": \"Brief workflow description\",',\n        '    \"confidence\": 0.85',\n        \"  }\",\n        \"]\",\n        \"```\",\n        \"\",\n        \"## Important Constraints\",\n        \"\",\n        \"- Only reference files that exist in the evidence\",\n        \"- Do not generate code patches\",\n        \"- Do not claim tests pass without verification\",\n        \"- Mark confidence honestly (0.0 to 1.0)\",\n        \"- Focus on high-impact improvements\",\n        \"\",\n    ]\n    \n    return \"\\n\".join(lines)\n\n\ndef parse_ai_recommendations(\n    response_text: str,\n    snapshot: RepositorySnapshot,\n) -> list[CodeRecommendation]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Parse AI response into CodeRecommendation objects.\n\n    Args:\n        response_text: Raw AI response text\n        snapshot: Repository snapshot for validation\n\n    Returns:\n        List of parsed and validated recommendations\n    \"\"\"\n    import json\n    import re\n    \n    recommendations = []\n    \n    # Try to extract JSON from response\n    # Look for JSON array in code blocks or raw text\n    json_match = re.search(r'```(?:json)?\\s*(\\[.*?\\])\\s*```', response_text, re.DOTALL)\n    if json_match:\n        json_text = json_match.group(1)\n    else:\n        # Try to find raw JSON array\n        json_match = re.search(r'\\[.*?\\]', response_text, re.DOTALL)\n        if json_match:\n            json_text = json_match.group(0)\n        else:\n            return []\n    \n    try:\n        data = json.loads(json_text)\n        if not isinstance(data, list):\n            return []\n        \n        for item in data:\n            if not isinstance(item, dict):\n                continue\n            \n            try:\n                rec = CodeRecommendation.from_dict(item)\n                recommendations.append(rec)\n            except (KeyError, ValueError, TypeError):\n                continue\n    \n    except json.JSONDecodeError:\n        return []\n    \n    # Validate all recommendations\n    return validate_recommendations(recommendations, snapshot)\n\n\ndef generate_recommendation_summary(result: AIRecommendationResult) -> str:\n    \"\"\"\n    Generate a text summary of recommendations.\n\n    Args:\n        result: AI recommendation result\n\n    Returns:\n        Markdown summary string\n    \"\"\"\n    lines = [\n        \"# AI Code Recommendations\",\n        \"\",\n        f\"**Provider**: {result.provider}\",\n        f\"**Model**: {result.model}\",\n        f\"**Generated**: {result.timestamp}\",\n        \"\",\n        f\"**Total Recommendations**: {len(result.recommendations)}\",\n        f\"**Valid**: {len(result.valid_recommendations)}\",\n        f\"**Trusted**: {len(result.trusted_recommendations)}\",\n        \"\",\n    ]\n    \n    if result.warnings:\n        lines.extend([\n            \"## Warnings\",\n            \"\",\n        ])\n        for warning in result.warnings:\n            lines.append(f\"- {warning}\")\n        lines.append(\"\")\n    \n    # Group by priority\n    high_priority = [r for r in result.trusted_recommendations if r.priority == \"high\"]\n    medium_priority = [r for r in result.trusted_recommendations if r.priority == \"medium\"]\n    low_priority = [r for r in result.trusted_recommendations if r.priority == \"low\"]\n    \n    if high_priority:\n        lines.extend([\n            \"## High Priority\",\n            \"\",\n        ])\n        for rec in high_priority:\n            lines.append(f\"- **{rec.title}** ({rec.category})\")\n        lines.append(\"\")\n    \n    if medium_priority:\n        lines.extend([\n            \"## Medium Priority\",\n            \"\",\n        ])\n        for rec in medium_priority:\n            lines.append(f\"- **{rec.title}** ({rec.category})\")\n        lines.append(\"\")\n    \n    if low_priority:\n        lines.extend([\n            \"## Low Priority\",\n            \"\",\n        ])\n        for rec in low_priority:\n            lines.append(f\"- **{rec.title}** ({rec.category})\")\n        lines.append(\"\")\n    \n    return \"\\n\".join(lines)\n"
    },

Assistant is disabled. Enable it by setting REPOQUEST_AI_ENABLED=true and providing a CLAUDE_API_KEY.\",\n    )\n\n\nclass MockAssistantProvider:\n  \"\"\"Mock provider for testing.\"\"\"\n\n  def __init__(self, mock_response: str = \"This is a mock AI response.\", mock_citations: list[AssistantCitation] | None = None):\n    self.mock_response = mock_response\n    self.mock_citations = mock_citations or []\n\n  def generate(self, request: AssistantRequest) -> AssistantResponse:\n    \"\"\"Return a mock response.\"\"\"\n    return AssistantResponse(\n      status=\"ok\",\n      response_text=self.mock_response,\n      citations=self.mock_citations,\n      provider=\"mock\",\n      model=\"mock-model\",\n    )\n\n\nclass AssistantServiceProvider:\n  \"\"\"Provider that submits requests to an asynchronous assistant service.\"\"\"\n\n  def __init__(self, service_url: str, timeout_seconds: float = DEFAULT_SERVICE_TIMEOUT_SECONDS):\n    self.service_url = service_url.rstrip(\"/\")\n    self.timeout_seconds = timeout_seconds\n\n  def generate(self, request: AssistantRequest) -> AssistantResponse:\n    \"\"\"Submit a request to the service and poll until it completes.\"\"\"\n    if not self.service_url:\n      return AssistantResponse(\n        status=\"error\",\n        response_text=\"\",\n        provider=\"assistant-service\",\n        model=\"remote\",\n        message=\"REPOQUEST_ASSISTANT_SERVICE_URL is not configured.\",\n      )\n\n    try:\n      job_id = self._submit_job(request)\n      return self._wait_for_job(job_id)\n    except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:\n      body = e.read().decode(\"utf-8\", errors=\"ignore\") if e.fp else \"\"\n      return AssistantResponse(\n        status=\"error\",\n        response_text=\"\",\n        provider=\"assistant-service\",\n        model=\"remote\",\n        message=f\"Assistant service error ({e.code}): {body[:200]}\",\n      )\n    except urllib.error.URLError as e:\n      return AssistantResponse(\n        status=\"error\",\n        response_text=\"\",\n        provider=\"assistant-service\",\n        model=\"remote\",\n        message=f\"Assistant service network error: {str(e)}\",\n      )\n    except TimeoutError as e:\n      return AssistantResponse(\n        status=\"error\",\n        response_text=\"\",\n        provider=\"assistant-service\",\n        model=\"remote\",\n        message=str(e),\n      )\n    except (ValueError, json.JSONDecodeError) as e:\n      return AssistantResponse(\n        status=\"error\",\n        response_text=\"\",\n        provider=\"assistant-service\",\n        model=\"remote\",\n        message=f\"Invalid assistant service response: {str(e)}\",\n      )\n\n  def _submit_job(self, request: AssistantRequest) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Create a service job and return its id.\"\"\"\n    payload = json.dumps({\"request\": request.to_dict()}).encode(\"utf-8\")\n    http_request = urllib.request.Request(\n      f\"{self.service_url}/jobs\",\n      data=payload,\n      headers={\"Content-Type\": \"application/json\"},\n      method=\"POST\",\n    )\n\n    with urllib.request.urlopen(http_request, timeout=10) as response:\n      data = json.loads(response.read().decode(\"utf-8\"))\n\n    job_id = str(data.get(\"job_id\", \"\"))\n    if not job_id:\n      raise ValueError(\"assistant service did not return a job_id\")\n    return job_id\n\n  def _wait_for_job(self, job_id: str) -> AssistantResponse:\n    \"\"\"Poll service job status until a response is ready.\"\"\"\n    deadline = time.monotonic() + self.timeout_seconds\n\n    while time.monotonic() < deadline:\n      status = self._get_job_status(job_id)\n      if status.is_terminal:\n        if status.response:\n          return status.response\n        return AssistantResponse(\n          status=\"error\",\n          response_text=\"\",\n          provider=\"assistant-service\",\n          model=\"remote\",\n          message=status.message or f\"Assistant service job {job_id} finished without a response.\",\n        )\n      time.sleep(DEFAULT_SERVICE_POLL_INTERVAL_SECONDS)\n\n    raise TimeoutError(\n      f\"Assistant service job {job_id} timed out after {self.timeout_seconds:.0f}s.\"\n    )\n\n  def _get_job_status(self, job_id: str) -> AssistantJobStatus:\n    \"\"\"Fetch current job status from the service.\"\"\"\n    with urllib.request.urlopen(f\"{self.service_url}/jobs/{job_id}\", timeout=10) as response:\n      data = json.loads(response.read().decode(\"utf-8\"))\n\n    if not isinstance(data, dict):\n      raise ValueError(\"assistant service returned a non-object job status\")\n    return AssistantJobStatus.from_dict(data)\n\n\nclass LocalModelAssistantProvider:\n  \"\"\"Provider using OpenAI-compatible local model endpoints.\"\"\"\n\n  def __init__(self, base_url: str | None = None, model: str | None = None):\n    _, _, _, _, configured_base_url, configured_model = get_assistant_config()\n    self.base_url = (base_url if base_url is not None else configured_base_url).rstrip(\"/\")\n    self.model = model if model is not None else configured_model\n    self.ssl_context = _build_ssl_context()\n\n  def generate(self, request: AssistantRequest) -> AssistantResponse:\n    \"\"\"Generate a response using OpenAI-compatible local model API.\"\"\"\n    if not self.base_url:\n      return AssistantResponse(\n        status=\"error\",\n        response_text=\"\",\n        provider=\"local\",\n        model=self.model or \"unknown\",\n        message=\"REPOQUEST_LOCAL_MODEL_BASE_URL is not configured.\",\n      )\n\n    if not self.model:\n      return AssistantResponse(\n        status=\"error\",\n        response_text=\"\",\n        provider=\"local\",\n        model=\"unknown\",\n        message=\"REPOQUEST_LOCAL_MODEL_NAME is not configured.\",\n      )\n\n    try:\n      # Build system message with safety instructions\n      system_message = \"\"\"You are a code analysis assistant for RepoQuest, a static repository analyzer.\n\nIMPORTANT RULES:\n1. Use ONLY the provided context and code snippets\n2. Cite specific file paths when making file-specific claims\n3. Label uncertainty clearly (e.g., \"likely\", \"appears to\", \"suggests\")\n4. Do NOT claim that tests were run or executed\n5. Do NOT instruct RepoQuest to execute uploaded code\n6. Do NOT install dependencies or run commands\n7. This is STATIC ANALYSIS ONLY - no code execution occurred\n\nYour response should be:\n- Concise and actionable\n- Evidence-based with file path citations\n- Honest about limitations\n- Focused on the user's goal\"\"\"\n\n      # Build user message from request\n      user_message = request.to_prompt()\n\n      # Prepare OpenAI-compatible API request\n      data = {\n        \"model\": self.model,\n        \"messages\": [\n          {\n            \"role\": \"system\",\n            \"content\": system_message\n          },\n          {\n            \"role\": \"user\",\n            \"content\": user_message\n          }\n        ],\n        \"max_tokens\": request.max_tokens,\n        \"temperature\": 0.7,\n      }\n\n      # Make API request to OpenAI-compatible endpoint\n      api_url = f\"{self.base_url}/chat/completions\"\n      req = urllib.request.Request(\n        api_url,\n        data=json.dumps(data).encode('utf-8'),\n        headers={\n          \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\",\n        },\n        method=\"POST\"\n      )\n\n      with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=60, context=self.ssl_context) as response:\n        result = json.loads(response.read().decode('utf-8'))\n\n      # Extract response text from OpenAI-compatible format\n      if \"choices\" in result and len(result[\"choices\"]) > 0:\n        choice = result[\"choices\"][0]\n        if \"message\" in choice and \"content\" in choice[\"message\"]:\n          response_text = choice[\"message\"][\"content\"]\n        else:\n          response_text = \"\"\n      else:\n        response_text = \"\"\n\n      if not response_text:\n        return AssistantResponse(\n          status=\"error\",\n          response_text=\"\",\n          provider=\"local\",\n          model=self.model,\n          message=\"Empty response from local model API\",\n        )\n\n      # Extract citations from response\n      citations = self._extract_citations(response_text, request.evidence_files)\n\n      return AssistantResponse(\n        status=\"ok\",\n        response_text=response_text,\n        citations=citations,\n        provider=\"local\",\n        model=self.model,\n      )\n\n    except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:\n      error_body = e.read().decode('utf-8', errors='ignore') if e.fp else \"\"\n      return AssistantResponse(\n        status=\"error\",\n        response_text=\"\",\n        provider=\"local\",\n        model=self.model,\n        message=f\"Local model API error ({e.code}): {error_body[:200]}\",\n      )\n\n    except urllib.error.URLError as e:\n      if _is_certificate_verification_error(e):\n        return AssistantResponse(\n          status=\"error\",\n          response_text=\"\",\n          provider=\"local\",\n          model=self.model,\n          message=CERTIFICATE_ERROR_MESSAGE,\n        )\n      return AssistantResponse(\n        status=\"error\",\n        response_text=\"\",\n        provider=\"local\",\n        model=self.model,\n        message=f\"Network error connecting to local model: {str(e)}\",\n      )\n\n    except Exception as e:\n      if _is_certificate_verification_error(e):\n        return AssistantResponse(\n          status=\"error\",\n          response_text=\"\",\n          provider=\"local\",\n          model=self.model,\n          message=CERTIFICATE_ERROR_MESSAGE,\n        )\n      return AssistantResponse(\n        status=\"error\",\n        response_text=\"\",\n        provider=\"local\",\n        model=self.model,\n        message=f\"Unexpected error: {str(e)}\",\n      )\n\n  def _extract_citations(self, response_text: str, evidence_files: list[str]) -> list[AssistantCitation]:\n    \"\"\"Extract file path citations from response text.\"\"\"\n    citations = []\n\n    # Look for file paths mentioned in the response\n    for file_path in evidence_files:\n      if file_path in response_text or f\"`{file_path}`\" in response_text:\n        citations.append(AssistantCitation(\n          file_path=file_path,\n          relevance=\"mentioned in response\"\n        ))\n\n    return citations\n\n\nclass ClaudeAssistantProvider:\n  \"\"\"Provider using Claude API via Anthropic Messages API.\"\"\"\n\n  def __init__(self, api_key: str | None = None, model: str | None = None):\n    _, _, configured_api_key, configured_model, _, _ = get_assistant_config()\n    self.api_key = api_key if api_key is not None else configured_api_key\n    self.model = model if model is not None else configured_model\n    self.api_url = \"https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages\"\n    self.api_version = \"2023-06-01\"\n    self.ssl_context = _build_ssl_context()\n\n  def generate(self, request: AssistantRequest) -> AssistantResponse:\n    \"\"\"Generate a response using Claude API.\"\"\"\n    if not self.api_key:\n      return AssistantResponse(\n        status=\"error\",\n        response_text=\"\",\n        provider=\"claude\",\n        model=self.model,\n        message=\"CLAUDE_API_KEY is not configured.\",\n      )\n\n    try:\n      # Build system message with safety instructions\n      system_message = \"\"\"You are a code analysis assistant for RepoQuest, a static repository analyzer.\n\nIMPORTANT RULES:\n1. Use ONLY the provided context and code snippets\n2. Cite specific file paths when making file-specific claims\n3. Label uncertainty clearly (e.g., \"likely\", \"appears to\", \"suggests\")\n4. Do NOT claim that tests were run or executed\n5. Do NOT instruct RepoQuest to execute uploaded code\n6. Do NOT install dependencies or run commands\n7. This is STATIC ANALYSIS ONLY - no code execution occurred\n\nYour response should be:\n- Concise and actionable\n- Evidence-based with file path citations\n- Honest about limitations\n- Focused on the user's goal\"\"\"\n\n      # Build user message from request\n      user_message = request.to_prompt()\n\n      # Prepare API request\n      data = {\n        \"model\": self.model,\n        \"max_tokens\": request.max_tokens,\n        \"system\": system_message,\n        \"messages\": [\n          {\n            \"role\": \"user\",\n            \"content\": user_message\n          }\n        ]\n      }\n\n      # Make API request\n      req = urllib.request.Request(\n        self.api_url,\n        data=json.dumps(data).encode('utf-8'),\n        headers={\n          \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\",\n          \"x-api-key\": self.api_key,\n          \"anthropic-version\": self.api_version,\n        },\n        method=\"POST\"\n      )\n\n      with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=30, context=self.ssl_context) as response:\n        result = json.loads(response.read().decode('utf-8'))\n\n      # Extract response text\n      if \"content\" in result and len(result[\"content\"]) > 0:\n        response_text = result[\"content\"][0].get(\"text\", \"\")\n      else:\n        response_text = \"\"\n\n      if not response_text:\n        return AssistantResponse(\n          status=\"error\",\n          response_text=\"\",\n          provider=\"claude\",\n          model=self.model,\n          message=\"Empty response from Claude API\",\n        )\n\n      # Extract citations from response (simple heuristic: look for file paths in backticks)\n      citations = self._extract_citations(response_text, request.evidence_files)\n\n      return AssistantResponse(\n        status=\"ok\",\n        response_text=response_text,\n        citations=citations,\n        provider=\"claude\",\n        model=self.model,\n      )\n\n    except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:\n      error_body = e.read().decode('utf-8') if e.fp else \"\"\n      return AssistantResponse(\n        status=\"error\",\n        response_text=\"\",\n        provider=\"claude\",\n        model=self.model,\n        message=f\"Claude API error ({e.code}): {error_body[:200]}\",\n      )\n\n    except urllib.error.URLError as e:\n      if _is_certificate_verification_error(e):\n        return AssistantResponse(\n          status=\"error\",\n          response_text=\"\",\n          provider=\"claude\",\n          model=self.model,\n          message=CERTIFICATE_ERROR_MESSAGE,\n        )\n      return AssistantResponse(\n        status=\"error\",\n        response_text=\"\",\n        provider=\"claude\",\n        model=self.model,\n        message=f\"Network error: {str(e)}\",\n      )\n\n    except Exception as e:\n      if _is_certificate_verification_error(e):\n        return AssistantResponse(\n          status=\"error\",\n          response_text=\"\",\n          provider=\"claude\",\n          model=self.model,\n          message=CERTIFICATE_ERROR_MESSAGE,\n        )\n      return AssistantResponse(\n        status=\"error\",\n        response_text=\"\",\n        provider=\"claude\",\n        model=self.model,\n        message=f\"Unexpected error: {str(e)}\",\n      )\n\n  def _extract_citations(self, response_text: str, evidence_files: list[str]) -> list[AssistantCitation]:\n    \"\"\"Extract file path citations from response text.\"\"\"\n    citations = []\n\n    # Look for file paths mentioned in the response\n    for file_path in evidence_files:\n      if file_path in response_text or f\"`{file_path}`\" in response_text:\n        citations.append(AssistantCitation(\n          file_path=file_path,\n          relevance=\"mentioned in response\"\n        ))\n\n    return citations\n\n\ndef get_assistant_provider() -> AssistantProvider:\n  \"\"\"Get the appropriate assistant provider based on configuration.\"\"\"\n  ai_enabled, provider, api_key, model, local_base_url, local_model_name = get_assistant_config()\n  service_url, service_timeout = get_assistant_service_config()\n\n  if not ai_enabled:\n    return DisabledAssistantProvider()\n\n  # If service URL is configured, use the async service\n  if service_url:\n    return AssistantServiceProvider(service_url=service_url, timeout_seconds=service_timeout)\n\n  # Route to appropriate provider based on configuration\n  if provider == \"local\":\n    if not local_base_url or not local_model_name:\n      return DisabledAssistantProvider()\n    return LocalModelAssistantProvider(base_url=local_base_url, model=local_model_name)\n\n  # Default to Claude provider\n  if not api_key:\n    return DisabledAssistantProvider()\n\n  return ClaudeAssistantProvider(api_key=api_key, model=model)\n\n# Made with Bob\n"
          }
        }
      }
    ],

The tool's response will clearly indicate if truncation has occurred and will provide details on how to read more of the file using the 'offset' and 'limit' parameters. Handles text, images (PNG, JPG, GIF, WEBP, SVG, BMP), and PDF files. For text files, it can read specific line ranges.",

    "description": "Use to apply multiple PRECISE, TARGETED and SMALL modifications to one or more files by searching for specific sections of content and replacing them. apply_diff always operates on *whole* lines. the search must have exact same spacing, indentation and text. IMPORTANT: NEVER use apply_diff to replace large parts or whole file contents of a file!! use write_to_file instead.\n      \n      Diff format:\n      ```\n# Search: |||\n[exact content to find including whitespace]\n|||\n# Start Line: [optional line number where search should begin]\n# Replace with: |||\n[new content to replace with]\n|||\n# Purpose: [optional explanation of what this replacement does]\n\n# Search: |||\n[another exact content to find]\n|||\n# Start Line: [optional line number]\n# Replace with: |||\n[replacement content]\n|||\n```\n\nFor multiline search or replacements, use triple |||, use another if content contains that delimiter:\n\n```\n# Search: |||\nfunction example() {\n  const x = 1;\n  const y = 2;\n  return x + y;\n}\n|||\n# Start Line: 10\n# Replace with: |||\nfunction example() {\n  const x = 1;\n  const y = 2;\n  console.log('Computing sum');\n  return x + y;\n}\n|||\n# Purpose: Add logging to the function\n```\n\nExample:\n\nOriginal file:\n```\n1 | def calculate_total(items):\n2 |     total = 0\n3 |     for item in items:\n4 |         total += item\n5 |     return total\n```\n\nSearch/Replace content:\n```\n# Search: |||\ntotal += item\n|||\n# Start Line: 3\n# Replace with: |||\ntotal = total + item\n|||\n# Purpose: Make addition more explicit\n\n# Search: |||\ndef calculate_total(items):\n|||\n# Replace with: |||\ndef calculate_sum(items):\n|||\n# Purpose: Rename function to better reflect its purpose\n```\n\nUsage:\n<apply_diff>\n<file_path>File path here</file_path>\n<diff>\nYour search/replace content here\nYou can use multiple search/replace blocks in one diff block\n</diff>\n</apply_diff>\n",
    "renderOutputAsMarkdown": true
  },

This tool is designed for step-by-step task tracking, allowing you to confirm completion of each step before updating, update multiple task statuses at once (e.g., mark one as completed and start the next), and dynamically add new todos discovered during long or complex tasks.\n\n**Checklist Format:**\n- Use a single-level markdown checklist (no nesting or subtasks).\n- List todos in the intended execution order.\n- Status options:\n\t - [ ] Task description (pending)\n\t - [x] Task description (completed)\n\t - [-] Task description (in progress)\n\n**Status Rules:**\n- [ ] = pending (not started)\n- [x] = completed (fully finished, no unresolved issues)\n- [-] = in_progress (currently being worked on)\n\n**Core Principles:**\n- Before updating, always confirm which todos have been completed since the last update.\n- You may update multiple statuses in a single update (e.g., mark the previous as completed and the next as in progress).\n- When a new actionable item is discovered during a long or complex task, add it to the todo list immediately.\n- Do not remove any unfinished todos unless explicitly instructed.\n- Always retain all unfinished tasks, updating their status as needed.\n- Only mark a task as completed when it is fully accomplished (no partials, no unresolved dependencies).\n- If a task is blocked, keep it as in_progress and add a new todo describing what needs to be resolved.\n- Remove tasks only if they are no longer relevant or if the user requests deletion.\n\n**Usage Example:**\n<update_todo_list>\n<todos>\n[x] Analyze requirements\n[x] Design architecture\n[-] Implement core logic\n[ ] Write tests\n[ ] Update documentation\n</todos>\n</update_todo_list>\n\n*After completing \"Implement core logic\" and starting \"Write tests\":*\n<update_todo_list>\n<todos>\n[x] Analyze requirements\n[x] Design architecture\n[x] Implement core logic\n[-] Write tests\n[ ] Update documentation\n[ ] Add performance benchmarks\n</todos>\n</update_todo_list>\n\n**When to Use:**\n- The task is complicated or involves multiple steps or requires ongoing tracking.\n- You need to update the status of several todos at once.\n- New actionable items are discovered during task execution.\n- The user requests a todo list or provides multiple tasks.\n- The task is complex and benefits from clear, stepwise progress tracking.\n\n**When NOT to Use:**\n- There is only a single, trivial task.\n- The task can be completed in one or two simple steps.\n- The request is purely conversational or informational.\n\n**Task Management Guidelines:**\n- Mark task as completed immediately after all work of the current task is done.\n- Start the next task by marking it as in_progress.\n- Add new todos as soon as they are
